para_trans: Regarding transfers in the cadre of Deputy Superintendent Land Records (Group-B Gazetted).
GOVERNMENT ORDER, NUMBER: TRANSFER-0426 / QUESTION 137 / AASTA-6
Madam Kama Marg, Martyr Rajguru Chowk,

As per the approval given by the competent authority under the provisions of the Maharashtra Regulation and Prevention of Transfer of Government Employees Act, 2005, the Deputy Superintendent of Land for the convenience of the Administrator.
The following officers of the Records (Group-B Gazetted) Cadre are ranked No. 4 The post mentioned herein is being transferred and posted as follows: -
(2) The said order comes into force with immediate effect and the aforesaid officer is hereby transferred from the post he is holding to join the post indicated in the order with immediate effect.
Unilateral work is being done. The concerned officer shall preside over the office of his office.
It is necessary to join in time.

3. The Commissioner of Deposits and the Director of Land Records (MDLR) shall be informed of the date on which the officer concerned joined the place of new posting. State), Pune to the Government of doe6.rfd @ Maharashtra. Notify immediately at the email address gov.in.
4. Leave of the officer concerned shall not be granted for any reason except with the previous sanction of the Government.
Also, if any leave applications of officers transferred by the original office as per Rule-30 (c) of the MNS (Leave) Rules, 1981 are received, they shall not be entertained.

The officer must return the registration to the original address and the payment by post. If applications for leave are sent by officers transferred to the parent office, these applications will not be considered.

5. In case of non-joining of the place of new posting immediately under the said transfer order or any attempt to cancel the transfer under any kind of pressure, the concerned shall be liable to be dealt with as misconduct as the act violates Rule 23 of the Maharashtra Civil Services (Conduct) Rules, 1979.
The Government Order is available on the Government of Maharashtra website www.maharashtra.gov.in.
